one_line_ask: Take the client's supplied product/founder shots, raster logo, and copy/hex palette and turn them into a cohesive, Instagram-ready brand asset pack: clean cutouts, one unified color grade across every image, exact platform sizes (1080×1920 reel + story, 1080×1350 feed), plus the logo as a scalable vector (AI/SVG) and transparent PNG.
brief: We are a B2B SaaS startup refreshing our Instagram presence and need the client-supplied source assets cleaned up, color-matched, and exported into a cohesive, platform-correct brand pack. At kickoff we hand you: (1) a square studio product shot of our app running on a phone-in-hand, (2) a founder/team candid for an 'about us' Reel cover, (3) our existing logo as a flat raster PNG that we've lost the vector for, and our brand palette — primary indigo #4F46E5, ink #0F172A, off-white #F8FAFC. Your job (production prep only — no new layout design, no type setting): license a clean gradient/studio backdrop from stock; cut our product and founder out of their busy original backgrounds; place each subject on the brand off-white; then push EVERY image through ONE identical brand grade (auto-tone baseline, then a cooler indigo-leaning temperature, lifted shadows, and a controlled vibrance bump) so the product shot, the founder cover, and the stock backdrop all read as one family. Because our hero product shot is square but the Reel cover must be a tall 1080×1920, generatively expand its short axis first so nothing important gets cropped, then deliver platform-exact exports: a 1080×1920 Ree
